questions.question: How does dance function as behavioral activation for depression?

options.text: Dance has nothing to do with behavioral activation
Dance is inherently rewarding (music, movement, social connection) — not just 'good for you' — which breaks depression's avoidance pattern and re-engages you with pleasurable, structured activities
Only talk therapy counts as behavioral activation

questions.explanation: Behavioral Activation (BA) is an evidence-based therapy for depression: systematically re-engage with rewarding activities that depression causes you to avoid. Dance IS behavioral activation because it's inherently pleasurable (music triggers dopamine, movement is rewarding, social connection counters isolation) — not just something you 'should' do. It breaks avoidance, restores routines, provides mastery experiences, and offers low-pressure social re-engagement.

questions.question: What is the synergistic effect of combining music with movement for mental health?

options.text: Music has no effect on exercise benefits
Music triggers dopamine release in the brain's reward system; exercise also triggers dopamine — combining them creates an additive dopamine effect for stronger mood boost than either alone
Music during exercise is distracting and harmful

questions.explanation: Music activates the brain's reward system (dopamine release). Exercise also triggers dopamine. Music + movement = synergistic/additive effect — stronger mood boost than movement alone. Additionally, music provides rhythmic entrainment (your body synchronizes to the beat, regulating heart rate and nervous system), distraction from discomfort, and emotional expression outlet. This is why dance feels more rewarding than silent treadmill running for many people.

questions.question: What is the difference between performance-oriented dance (choreographed classes) and expressive dance (ecstatic dance, 5Rhythms)?

options.text: There is no difference — all dance is the same

Performance-oriented dance focuses on skill mastery and structured choreography; expressive dance is free-form with no 'right way,' emphasizing authentic emotional expression and cathartic release without judgment
Expressive dance has no mental health benefits
Only professional dancers should do choreographed dance

questions.explanation: Performance-oriented dance (hip-hop classes, choreography studios, dance fitness) involves learning structured movements, skill development, and often some performance/comparison element. Expressive dance (ecstatic dance, 5Rhythms) is free-form movement with no choreography, no 'right way,' emphasis on moving however you feel — great for emotional release, embodiment, and processing feelings non-verbally. Different people need different approaches: mastery-oriented → choreography; need for emotional outlet → expressive.

questions.question: Why is novelty particularly important for ADHD brains, and how does dance provide it?

options.text: ADHD brains don't need novelty at all
ADHD brains crave novelty to sustain attention; dance provides constant novelty through changing choreography, music variety, and social dynamics — unlike repetitive cardio
Only medication works for ADHD, not exercise

questions.explanation: ADHD brains have difficulty sustaining attention on repetitive tasks (like treadmill running 30 min staring at a wall). They crave novelty. Dance provides high novelty: choreography changes weekly, music variety (different songs, tempos, genres), social dynamics (different people, interactions), creative expression (interpreting music your way). This sustained novelty keeps ADHD brains engaged in ways that repetitive cardio often doesn't.

questions.question: Create a 3-song 'movement playlist' for this week. Choose songs that make you want to move. What songs did you choose, and what emotional state or energy do they represent? Commit to dancing to this playlist (at home, alone, no judgment) 3x this week.
questions.explanation: This reflection applies the lesson's emphasis on starting private (reducing performance anxiety) and using music as a motivational tool. Your playlist choices reveal what resonates with you: upbeat high-BPM for energy/motivation? Rhythmic steady-BPM for grounding? Favorite songs for emotional connection/nostalgia? The commitment to dance alone 3x reduces the barrier — you're just moving to music you love, no rules, no judgment. This builds the habit in a safe context.

title: Dance and Creative Movement Therapy - Quiz
